mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable consumer prices indicates a positive supply shock that expands profit margins without triggering inflation fears, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly from the baseline when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase reflects renewed private sector confidence and construction activity, which transmits to broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations, or the signal fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index directly signals expanding real output capacity, which improves corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative direction in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year sample, or the predictive correlation with 5-day forward returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above a 2.5 percent annualized rate, it forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses the 2.5% threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that sustains aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real personal savings during a credit tightening cycle signals reduced household financial buffers, which constrains future consumption and corporate revenues, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following RSAFS declines, or the signal shows no predictive power when controlling for FEDFUNDS movements.
